column4: And thou shalt command the children of {{c1::Israel}}, that they bring unto thee pure olive oil beaten for the light, to cause a lamp to burn continually.
And thou shalt {{c1::command}} the children of Israel, that they bring unto thee pure olive oil beaten for the light, to cause a lamp to burn continually.

In the tent of meeting, without the veil which is before the testimony, {{c1::Aaron}} and his sons shall set it in order, to burn from evening to morning before the LORD; it shall be a statute for ever throughout their generations on the behalf of the children of Israel.

In the tent of meeting, without the veil which is before the testimony, Aaron and his sons shall set it in order, to burn from evening to morning before the LORD; it shall be a statute for ever throughout their generations on the behalf of the children of {{c1::Israel}}.

And bring thou near unto thee {{c1::Aaron}} thy brother, and his sons with him, from among the children of Israel, that they may minister unto Me in the priest’s office, even Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar, Aaron’s sons.

And bring thou near unto thee Aaron thy brother, and his sons with him, from among the children of {{c1::Israel}}, that they may minister unto Me in the priest’s office, even Aaron, Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar, Aaron’s sons.
And thou shalt make holy garments for {{c1::Aaron}} thy brother, for splendour and for beauty.
And thou shalt make holy {{c1::garments}} for Aaron thy brother, for splendour and for beauty.

And thou shalt speak unto all that are wise-hearted, whom {{c1::I}} have filled with the spirit of wisdom, that they make Aaron’s garments to sanctify him, that he may minister unto Me in the priest’s office.

And thou shalt speak unto all that are wise-hearted, whom I have filled with the spirit of wisdom, that they make {{c1::Aaron’s}} garments to sanctify him, that he may minister unto Me in the priest’s office.

And these are the garments which they shall make: a breastplate, and an ephod, and a robe, and a tunic of chequer work, a mitre, and a girdle; and they shall make holy garments for {{c1::Aaron}} thy brother, and his sons, that he may minister unto Me in the priest’s office.

And these are the garments which they shall make: a breastplate, and an ephod, and a robe, and a tunic of chequer work, a mitre, and a girdle; and they shall make holy garments for Aaron thy brother, and his sons, that he may minister unto {{c1::Me}} in the priest’s office.
And they shall take the gold, and the blue, and the {{c1::purple}}, and the scarlet, and the fine linen.
And they shall take the gold, and the blue, and the purple, and the {{c1::scarlet}}, and the fine linen.
And they shall make the ephod of gold, of blue, and {{c1::purple}}, scarlet, and fine twined linen, the work of the skilful workman.
And they shall make the ephod of gold, of blue, and purple, {{c1::scarlet}}, and fine twined linen, the work of the skilful workman.
It shall have two {{c1::shoulder}}-pieces joined to the two ends thereof, that it may be joined together.
It shall have two shoulder-{{c1::pieces}} joined to the two ends thereof, that it may be joined together.

And the {{c1::skilfully}} woven band, which is upon it, wherewith to gird it on, shall be like the work thereof and of the same piece: of gold, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen.

And the skilfully woven band, which is upon it, {{c1::wherewith}} to gird it on, shall be like the work thereof and of the same piece: of gold, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen.
And thou shalt take two onyx stones, and grave on them the names of the children of {{c1::Israel}}:
And thou shalt take two onyx {{c1::stones}}, and grave on them the names of the children of Israel:
six of their names on the one stone, and the names of the six that {{c1::remain}} on the other stone, according to their birth.
six of their names on the one stone, and the names of the six that remain on the other stone, {{c1::according}} to their birth.

With the work of an engraver in stone, like the engravings of a signet, shalt thou engrave the two stones, according to the names of the children of {{c1::Israel}}; thou shalt make them to be inclosed in settings of gold.

With the work of an {{c1::engraver}} in stone, like the engravings of a signet, shalt thou engrave the two stones, according to the names of the children of Israel; thou shalt make them to be inclosed in settings of gold.

And thou shalt put the two stones upon the shoulder-pieces of the ephod, to be stones of memorial for the children of {{c1::Israel}}; and Aaron shall bear their names before the LORD upon his two shoulders for a memorial.

And thou shalt put the two stones upon the shoulder-pieces of the ephod, to be stones of memorial for the children of Israel; and {{c1::Aaron}} shall bear their names before the LORD upon his two shoulders for a memorial.
And thou shalt make {{c1::settings}} of gold;
and two {{c1::chains}} of pure gold; of plaited thread shalt thou make them, of wreathen work; and thou shalt put the wreathen chains on the settings.
and two chains of pure gold; of {{c1::plaited}} thread shalt thou make them, of wreathen work; and thou shalt put the wreathen chains on the settings.

And thou shalt make a {{c1::breastplate}} of judgment, the work of the skilful workman; like the work of the ephod thou shalt make it: of gold, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, shalt thou make it.

And thou shalt make a breastplate of {{c1::judgment}}, the work of the skilful workman; like the work of the ephod thou shalt make it: of gold, of blue, and purple, and scarlet, and fine twined linen, shalt thou make it.
Four-{{c1::square}} it shall be and double: a span shall be the length thereof, and a span the breadth thereof.
Four-square it shall be and {{c1::double}}: a span shall be the length thereof, and a span the breadth thereof.
And thou shalt set in it {{c1::settings}} of stones, four rows of stones: a row of carnelian, topaz, and smaragd shall be the first row;
And thou shalt set in it settings of {{c1::stones}}, four rows of stones: a row of carnelian, topaz, and smaragd shall be the first row;
and the {{c1::second}} row a carbuncle, a sapphire, and an emerald;
and the second row a {{c1::carbuncle}}, a sapphire, and an emerald;
and the third row a {{c1::jacinth}}, an agate, and an amethyst;
and the third row a jacinth, an agate, and an {{c1::amethyst}};
and the {{c1::fourth}} row a beryl, and an onyx, and a jasper; they shall be inclosed in gold in their settings.
and the fourth row a beryl, and an onyx, and a {{c1::jasper}}; they shall be inclosed in gold in their settings.

And the stones shall be according to the names of the children of {{c1::Israel}}, twelve, according to their names; like the engravings of a signet, every one according to his name, they shall be for the twelve tribes.

And the {{c1::stones}} shall be according to the names of the children of Israel, twelve, according to their names; like the engravings of a signet, every one according to his name, they shall be for the twelve tribes.
And thou shalt make upon the {{c1::breastplate}} plaited chains of wreathen work of pure gold.
And thou shalt make upon the breastplate {{c1::plaited}} chains of wreathen work of pure gold.
And thou shalt make upon the {{c1::breastplate}} two rings of gold, and shalt put the two rings on the two ends of the breastplate.
And thou shalt put the two {{c1::wreathen}} chains of gold on the two rings at the ends of the breastplate.
And thou shalt put the two wreathen {{c1::chains}} of gold on the two rings at the ends of the breastplate.
And the other two ends of the two {{c1::wreathen}} chains thou shalt put on the two settings, and put them on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod, in the forepart thereof.
And the other two ends of the two wreathen {{c1::chains}} thou shalt put on the two settings, and put them on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod, in the forepart thereof.
And thou shalt make two rings of gold, and thou shalt put them upon the two ends of the {{c1::breastplate}}, upon the edge thereof, which is toward the side of the ephod inward.
And thou shalt make two rings of gold, and thou shalt put them upon the two ends of the breastplate, upon the edge {{c1::thereof}}, which is toward the side of the ephod inward.

And thou shalt make two rings of gold, and shalt put them on the two {{c1::shoulder}}-pieces of the ephod underneath, in the forepart thereof, close by the coupling thereof, above the skilfully woven band of the ephod.

And thou shalt make two rings of gold, and shalt put them on the two shoulder-{{c1::pieces}} of the ephod underneath, in the forepart thereof, close by the coupling thereof, above the skilfully woven band of the ephod.

And they shall bind the {{c1::breastplate}} by the rings thereof unto the rings of the ephod with a thread of blue, that it may be upon the skilfully woven band of the ephod, and that the breastplate be not loosed from the ephod.

And they shall bind the breastplate by the rings {{c1::thereof}} unto the rings of the ephod with a thread of blue, that it may be upon the skilfully woven band of the ephod, and that the breastplate be not loosed from the ephod.
And {{c1::Aaron}} shall bear the names of the children of Israel in the breastplate of judgment upon his heart, when he goeth in unto the holy place, for a memorial before the LORD continually. .
And Aaron shall bear the names of the children of {{c1::Israel}} in the breastplate of judgment upon his heart, when he goeth in unto the holy place, for a memorial before the LORD continually. .

And thou shalt put in the breastplate of judgment the {{c1::Urim}} and the Thummim; and they shall be upon Aaron’s heart, when he goeth in before the LORD; and Aaron shall bear the judgment of the children of Israel upon his heart before the LORD continually.

And thou shalt put in the breastplate of judgment the Urim and the {{c1::Thummim}}; and they shall be upon Aaron’s heart, when he goeth in before the LORD; and Aaron shall bear the judgment of the children of Israel upon his heart before the LORD continually.
And it shall have a hole for the head in the midst {{c1::thereof}}; it shall have a binding of woven work round about the hole of it, as it were the hole of a coat of mail that it be not rent.
And it shall have a hole for the head in the midst thereof; it shall have a {{c1::binding}} of woven work round about the hole of it, as it were the hole of a coat of mail that it be not rent.
And upon the {{c1::skirts}} of it thou shalt make pomegranates of blue, and of purple, and of scarlet, round about the skirts thereof; and bells of gold between them round about:
And upon the skirts of it thou shalt make {{c1::pomegranates}} of blue, and of purple, and of scarlet, round about the skirts thereof; and bells of gold between them round about:
a {{c1::golden}} bell and a pomegranate, a golden bell and a pomegranate, upon the skirts of the robe round about.
a golden bell and a {{c1::pomegranate}}, a golden bell and a pomegranate, upon the skirts of the robe round about.
And it shall be upon {{c1::Aaron}} to minister; and the sound thereof shall be heard when he goeth in unto the holy place before the LORD, and when he cometh out, that he die not.
And it shall be upon Aaron to {{c1::minister}}; and the sound thereof shall be heard when he goeth in unto the holy place before the LORD, and when he cometh out, that he die not.
And thou shalt make a plate of pure gold, and engrave upon it, like the engravings of a signet: {{c1::HOLY}} TO THE LORD.
And thou shalt make a plate of pure gold, and {{c1::engrave}} upon it, like the engravings of a signet: HOLY TO THE LORD.
And thou shalt put it on a {{c1::thread}} of blue, and it shall be upon the mitre; upon the forefront of the mitre it shall be.
And thou shalt put it on a thread of blue, and it shall be upon the mitre; upon the {{c1::forefront}} of the mitre it shall be.

And it shall be upon {{c1::Aaron’s}} forehead, and Aaron shall bear the iniquity committed in the holy things, which the children of Israel shall hallow, even in all their holy gifts; and it shall be always upon his forehead, that they may be accepted before the LORD.

And it shall be upon {{c1::Aaron}}’s forehead, and Aaron shall bear the iniquity committed in the holy things, which the children of Israel shall hallow, even in all their holy gifts; and it shall be always upon his forehead, that they may be accepted before the LORD.
And thou shalt weave the tunic in {{c1::chequer}} work of fine linen, and thou shalt make a mitre of fine linen, and thou shalt make a girdle, the work of the weaver in colours.
And thou shalt weave the tunic in chequer work of fine linen, and thou shalt make a mitre of fine linen, and thou shalt make a {{c1::girdle}}, the work of the weaver in colours.
And for {{c1::Aaron’s}} sons thou shalt make tunics, and thou shalt make for them girdles, and head-tires shalt thou make for them, for splendour and for beauty.
And for Aaron’s sons thou shalt make {{c1::tunics}}, and thou shalt make for them girdles, and head-tires shalt thou make for them, for splendour and for beauty.

And thou shalt put them upon {{c1::Aaron}} thy brother, and upon his sons with him; and shalt anoint them, and consecrate them, and sanctify them, that they may minister unto Me in the priest’s office.

And thou shalt put them upon Aaron thy brother, and upon his sons with him; and shalt anoint them, and consecrate them, and sanctify them, that they may minister unto {{c1::Me}} in the priest’s office.
And thou shalt make them linen {{c1::breeches}} to cover the flesh of their nakedness; from the loins even unto the thighs they shall reach.
And thou shalt make them linen breeches to cover the flesh of their {{c1::nakedness}}; from the loins even unto the thighs they shall reach.

And they shall be upon {{c1::Aaron}}, and upon his sons, when they go in unto the tent of meeting, or when they come near unto the altar to minister in the holy place; that they bear not iniquity, and die; it shall be a statute for ever unto him and unto his seed after him.

And they shall be upon Aaron, and upon his sons, when they go in unto the tent of {{c1::meeting}}, or when they come near unto the altar to minister in the holy place; that they bear not iniquity, and die; it shall be a statute for ever unto him and unto his seed after him.
And this is the thing that thou shalt do unto them to hallow them, to minister unto {{c1::Me}} in the priest’s office: take one young bullock and two rams without blemish,
And this is the thing that thou shalt do unto them to {{c1::hallow}} them, to minister unto Me in the priest’s office: take one young bullock and two rams without blemish,
and {{c1::unleavened}} bread, and cakes unleavened mingled with oil, and wafers unleavened spread with oil; of fine wheaten flour shalt thou make them.
and unleavened bread, and cakes unleavened {{c1::mingled}} with oil, and wafers unleavened spread with oil; of fine wheaten flour shalt thou make them.
And thou shalt put them into one {{c1::basket}}, and bring them in the basket, with the bullock and the two rams.
And thou shalt put them into one basket, and bring them in the basket, with the {{c1::bullock}} and the two rams.
And {{c1::Aaron}} and his sons thou shalt bring unto the door of the tent of meeting, and shalt wash them with water.
And Aaron and his sons thou shalt bring unto the door of the tent of {{c1::meeting}}, and shalt wash them with water.
And thou shalt take the garments, and put upon {{c1::Aaron}} the tunic, and the robe of the ephod, and the ephod, and the breastplate, and gird him with the skilfully woven band of the ephod.
And thou shalt take the {{c1::garments}}, and put upon Aaron the tunic, and the robe of the ephod, and the ephod, and the breastplate, and gird him with the skilfully woven band of the ephod.
Then shalt thou take the {{c1::anointing}} oil, and pour it upon his head, and anoint him.
Then shalt thou take the anointing oil, and pour it upon his head, and {{c1::anoint}} him.
And thou shalt bring his sons, and put {{c1::tunics}} upon them.

And thou shalt gird them with girdles, {{c1::Aaron}} and his sons, and bind head-tires on them; and they shall have the priesthood by a perpetual statute; and thou shalt consecrate Aaron and his sons.

And thou shalt gird them with {{c1::girdles}}, Aaron and his sons, and bind head-tires on them; and they shall have the priesthood by a perpetual statute; and thou shalt consecrate Aaron and his sons.
And thou shalt bring the bullock before the tent of meeting; and {{c1::Aaron}} and his sons shall lay their hands upon the head of the bullock.
And thou shalt bring the {{c1::bullock}} before the tent of meeting; and Aaron and his sons shall lay their hands upon the head of the bullock.
And thou shalt kill the {{c1::bullock}} before the LORD, at the door of the tent of meeting.
And thou shalt kill the bullock {{c1::before}} the LORD, at the door of the tent of meeting.
And thou shalt take of the blood of the {{c1::bullock}}, and put it upon the horns of the altar with thy finger; and thou shalt pour out all the remaining blood at the base of the altar.
And thou shalt take of the blood of the bullock, and put it upon the horns of the altar with thy {{c1::finger}}; and thou shalt pour out all the remaining blood at the base of the altar.
And thou shalt take all the fat that {{c1::covereth}} the inwards, and the lobe above the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and make them smoke upon the altar.
And thou shalt take all the fat that covereth the {{c1::inwards}}, and the lobe above the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and make them smoke upon the altar.
But the flesh of the {{c1::bullock}}, and its skin, and its dung, shalt thou burn with fire without the camp; it is a sin-offering.
But the flesh of the bullock, and its skin, and its dung, shalt thou burn with fire {{c1::without}} the camp; it is a sin-offering.
Thou shalt also take the one ram; and {{c1::Aaron}} and his sons shall lay their hands upon the head of the ram.
And thou shalt slay the ram, and thou shalt take its blood, and dash it round about {{c1::against}} the altar.
And thou shalt cut the ram into its {{c1::pieces}}, and wash its inwards, and its legs, and put them with its pieces, and with its head.
And thou shalt cut the ram into its pieces, and wash its {{c1::inwards}}, and its legs, and put them with its pieces, and with its head.
And thou shalt make the whole ram smoke upon the altar; it is a burnt-{{c1::offering}} unto the LORD; it is a sweet savour, an offering made by fire unto the LORD.
And thou shalt make the whole ram smoke upon the altar; it is a burnt-offering unto the LORD; it is a sweet {{c1::savour}}, an offering made by fire unto the LORD.
And thou shalt take the other ram; and {{c1::Aaron}} and his sons shall lay their hands upon the head of the ram.

Then shalt thou kill the ram, and take of its blood, and put it upon the tip of the right ear of {{c1::Aaron}}, and upon the tip of the right ear of his sons, and upon the thumb of their right hand, and upon the great toe of their right foot, and dash the blood against the altar round about.

Then shalt thou kill the ram, and take of its blood, and put it upon the tip of the right ear of Aaron, and upon the tip of the right ear of his sons, and upon the thumb of their right hand, and upon the great toe of their right foot, and dash the blood {{c1::against}} the altar round about.

And thou shalt take of the blood that is upon the altar, and of the anointing oil, and sprinkle it upon {{c1::Aaron}}, and upon his garments, and upon his sons, and upon the garments of his sons with him; and he and his garments shall be hallowed, and his sons and his sons’garments with him.

And thou shalt take of the blood that is upon the altar, and of the {{c1::anointing}} oil, and sprinkle it upon Aaron, and upon his garments, and upon his sons, and upon the garments of his sons with him; and he and his garments shall be hallowed, and his sons and his sons’garments with him.

Also thou shalt take of the ram the fat, and the fat tail, and the fat that {{c1::covereth}} the inwards, and the lobe of the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and the right thigh; for it is a ram of consecration;

Also thou shalt take of the ram the fat, and the fat tail, and the fat that covereth the {{c1::inwards}}, and the lobe of the liver, and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, and the right thigh; for it is a ram of consecration;
and one loaf of bread, and one cake of oiled bread, and one wafer, out of the {{c1::basket}} of unleavened bread that is before the LORD.
and one loaf of bread, and one cake of oiled bread, and one wafer, out of the basket of {{c1::unleavened}} bread that is before the LORD.
And thou shalt put the whole upon the hands of {{c1::Aaron}}, and upon the hands of his sons; and shalt wave them for a wave-offering before the LORD.
And thou shalt put the whole upon the hands of Aaron, and upon the hands of his sons; and shalt wave them for a wave-{{c1::offering}} before the LORD.
And thou shalt take them from their hands, and make them smoke on the altar upon the burnt-{{c1::offering}}, for a sweet savour before the LORD; it is an offering made by fire unto the LORD.
And thou shalt take them from their hands, and make them smoke on the altar upon the burnt-offering, for a sweet {{c1::savour}} before the LORD; it is an offering made by fire unto the LORD.
And thou shalt take the breast of {{c1::Aaron’s}} ram of consecration, and wave it for a wave-offering before the LORD; and it shall be thy portion.
And thou shalt take the {{c1::breast}} of Aaron’s ram of consecration, and wave it for a wave-offering before the LORD; and it shall be thy portion.

And thou shalt sanctify the breast of the wave-offering, and the thigh of the heave-offering, which is waved, and which is heaved up, of the ram of consecration, even of that which is {{c1::Aaron’s}}, and of that which is his sons’.

And thou shalt {{c1::sanctify}} the breast of the wave-offering, and the thigh of the heave-offering, which is waved, and which is heaved up, of the ram of consecration, even of that which is Aaron’s, and of that which is his sons’.

And it shall be for {{c1::Aaron}} and his sons as a due for ever from the children of Israel; for it is a heave-offering; and it shall be a heave-offering from the children of Israel of their sacrifices of peace-offerings, even their heave-offering unto the LORD.

And it shall be for Aaron and his sons as a due for ever from the children of {{c1::Israel}}; for it is a heave-offering; and it shall be a heave-offering from the children of Israel of their sacrifices of peace-offerings, even their heave-offering unto the LORD.
And the holy garments of {{c1::Aaron}} shall be for his sons after him, to be anointed in them, and to be consecrated in them.
And the holy {{c1::garments}} of Aaron shall be for his sons after him, to be anointed in them, and to be consecrated in them.
Seven days shall the son that is {{c1::priest}} in his stead put them on, even he who cometh into the tent of meeting to minister in the holy place.
Seven days shall the son that is priest in his stead put them on, even he who {{c1::cometh}} into the tent of meeting to minister in the holy place.
And thou shalt take the ram of {{c1::consecration}}, and seethe its flesh in a holy place.
And thou shalt take the ram of consecration, and {{c1::seethe}} its flesh in a holy place.
And {{c1::Aaron}} and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram, and the bread that is in the basket, at the door of the tent of meeting.
And Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram, and the bread that is in the {{c1::basket}}, at the door of the tent of meeting.
And they shall eat those {{c1::things}} wherewith atonement was made, to consecrate and to sanctify them; but a stranger shall not eat thereof, because they are holy.
And they shall eat those things {{c1::wherewith}} atonement was made, to consecrate and to sanctify them; but a stranger shall not eat thereof, because they are holy.
And if aught of the flesh of the {{c1::consecration}}, or of the bread, remain unto the morning, then thou shalt burn the remainder with fire; it shall not be eaten, because it is holy.
And if aught of the flesh of the consecration, or of the bread, {{c1::remain}} unto the morning, then thou shalt burn the remainder with fire; it shall not be eaten, because it is holy.
And thus shalt thou do unto {{c1::Aaron}}, and to his sons, according to all that I have commanded thee; seven days shalt thou consecrate them.
And thus shalt thou do unto Aaron, and to his sons, according to all that {{c1::I}} have commanded thee; seven days shalt thou consecrate them.

And every day shalt thou offer the {{c1::bullock}} of sin-offering, beside the other offerings of atonement; and thou shalt do the purification upon the altar when thou makest atonement for it; and thou shalt anoint it, to sanctify it.

And every day shalt thou offer the bullock of sin-{{c1::offering}}, beside the other offerings of atonement; and thou shalt do the purification upon the altar when thou makest atonement for it; and thou shalt anoint it, to sanctify it.
Seven days thou shalt make {{c1::atonement}} for the altar, and sanctify it; thus shall the altar be most holy; whatsoever toucheth the altar shall be holy.
Seven days thou shalt make atonement for the altar, and {{c1::sanctify}} it; thus shall the altar be most holy; whatsoever toucheth the altar shall be holy.
Now this is that which thou shalt offer upon the altar: two lambs of the first year day by day {{c1::continually}}.
The one lamb thou shalt offer in the {{c1::morning}}; and the other lamb thou shalt offer at dusk.
And with the one lamb a tenth part of an ephah of fine flour {{c1::mingled}} with the fourth part of a hin of beaten oil; and the fourth part of a hin of wine for a drink-offering.
And with the one lamb a tenth part of an ephah of fine flour mingled with the {{c1::fourth}} part of a hin of beaten oil; and the fourth part of a hin of wine for a drink-offering.

And the other lamb thou shalt offer at dusk, and shalt do {{c1::thereto}} according to the meal-offering of the morning, and according to the drink-offering thereof, for a sweet savour, an offering made by fire unto the LORD.

And the other lamb thou shalt offer at dusk, and shalt do thereto {{c1::according}} to the meal-offering of the morning, and according to the drink-offering thereof, for a sweet savour, an offering made by fire unto the LORD.
It shall be a continual burnt-offering throughout your generations at the door of the tent of meeting before the LORD, where {{c1::I}} will meet with you, to speak there unto thee.
It shall be a {{c1::continual}} burnt-offering throughout your generations at the door of the tent of meeting before the LORD, where I will meet with you, to speak there unto thee.
And there {{c1::I}} will meet with the children of Israel; and [the Tent] shall be sanctified by My glory.
And there I will meet with the children of {{c1::Israel}}; and [the Tent] shall be sanctified by My glory.
And {{c1::I}} will sanctify the tent of meeting, and the altar; Aaron also and his sons will I sanctify, to minister to Me in the priest’s office.
And I will sanctify the tent of meeting, and the altar; {{c1::Aaron}} also and his sons will I sanctify, to minister to Me in the priest’s office.
And {{c1::I}} will dwell among the children of Israel, and will be their God.
And I will dwell among the children of {{c1::Israel}}, and will be their God.
And they shall know that {{c1::I}} am the LORD their God, that brought them forth out of the land of Egypt, that I may dwell among them. I am the LORD their God.
And they shall know that I am the LORD their God, that brought them forth out of the land of {{c1::Egypt}}, that I may dwell among them. I am the LORD their God.
And thou shalt make an altar to burn {{c1::incense}} upon; of acacia-wood shalt thou make it.
And thou shalt make an altar to burn incense upon; of {{c1::acacia}}-wood shalt thou make it.
A cubit shall be the {{c1::length}} thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof; foursquare shall it be; and two cubits shall be the height thereof; the horns thereof shall be of one piece with it.
A cubit shall be the length {{c1::thereof}}, and a cubit the breadth thereof; foursquare shall it be; and two cubits shall be the height thereof; the horns thereof shall be of one piece with it.
And thou shalt {{c1::overlay}} it with pure gold, the top thereof, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof; and thou shalt make unto it a crown of gold round about.
And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, the top {{c1::thereof}}, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof; and thou shalt make unto it a crown of gold round about.

And two {{c1::golden}} rings shalt thou make for it under the crown thereof, upon the two ribs thereof, upon the two sides of it shalt thou make them; and they shall be for places for staves wherewith to bear it.
